name: Mocha Spice Roll Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon ground cloves or to taste
1/4 cup superfine sugar plus to taste
recipeInstructions:
CAKE ROLL:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 10x15-inch jelly roll pan.
Line the pan with wax paper and grease the paper.
2. In the bowl of an electric mixer, beat the egg yolks and granulated
sugar together until the mixture is pale and fluffy.
3. Sift the cake flour, salt, baking powder, and spices. Add the dry
ingredients to the batter.
4. In a separate bowl, beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form.
Gently fold them into the batter.
5. Pour the batter into the prepared jelly roll pan. Bake for 20 minutes.
Remove from the oven and turn the cake over onto a clean linen dish
towel dusted with confectioners' sugar. Lift off the pan and let the
cake cool for 10 minutes. Peel off the wax paper from the cake.
Carefully roll the cake in a jelly roll fashion, and place it on a rack
to cool completely for 20-30 minutes.
6. When the cake is cooled, unroll to fill. Spoon a generous amount of
the mocha cream onto the inside of the cake, smoothing it within 1-inch
of all edges.
7. Roll the cake up to form a log. Place seam side down on a platter.
Spread the remaining mocha cream over the roll, frosting it evenly.
Run the tines of a fork along the top of the cake roll to create
decorative ridges resembling the bark of a tree. Refrigerate the cake
roll until serving time.
MOCHA CREAM FILLING AND FROSTING:
1. In a small saucepan, warm 1/2 pint of heavy cream with the instant
coffee just enough to dissolve the granules. Remove from heat and chill well.
2. In the bowl of an electric mixer, whip all of the cream, superfine
sugar, and cocoa until stiff peaks form. Refrigerate until needed.
